feat: Slack notifications for persistent failures (Phase 6)

Sends Slack notifications via incoming webhook when tests become
persistently failing (3+ consecutive). Includes:

- 10 unit tests

commit.message:
[feature][QSR] Add HW exception handling (#40654)

### Ticket
N/A

### Problem description
HW provides exceptions for illegal instruction etc. that weren't handle

### What's changed
Added an ISR to handle hardware exception that will set a WATCHER assert
if enabled. The assert will include the cause, the offending value and
the value of PC when it happened. ISR will also handle DFB related soft
interrupts - now also have an option to disable the handler if implicit
sync isn't needed.
NOTE: Added tests that force HW exceptions, for some reason only 2 types
were causing actual exceptions, couldn't get misaligned store/load or
load access fault to trigger.

tests](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/workflows/sanity-tests.yaml/badge.svg?branch=ayaacob/handle_errors)](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/workflows/sanity-tests.yaml?query=branch:ayaacob/handle_errors)

commit.message:
fix(mcp,api,task): memoize tool defs, explicit MCP shutdown, inline agent counts, resume-overlay routing

- Memoize base tool definitions via OnceLock in tool_definitions(); remove dead
  tool_definitions_chat_compat() wrapper and its #[allow(dead_code)]
- Add McpRegistry::shutdown() that explicitly cancels all connected MCP servers;
  wire through ConversationManager, RuntimeContext, facade.rs, and batch_mode.rs
  so servers are terminated on session end rather than relying on implicit drop
- Replace O(n) full-state-file scan in facade_list_agents with inline partial
  JSON reads via TaskState::load_live_summary() and TaskStateLiveSummary struct;
  extract state_files_in_dir(), state_file_path(), write_pretty_json_atomic()
  helpers in task_state.rs
- Fix resume-selection overlay routing: add explicit pending_resume_selection
  check in on_user_input before the catch-all approval handler so numeric input
  routes to task resume instead of approval
- Add dir field to ResumeTaskEntry for exact file loading; change
  list_recent_task_entries to accept working_dir parameter
- Use std::mem::take instead of drain().collect() (clippy::drain_collect)
- Add tests: resume_selection_overlay_routes_numeric_input_to_task_resume,
  test_live_session_task_counts_from_reads_inline_state_summary,
  test_live_session_task_counts_prefer_newest_copy_of_duplicate_task_ids

910 tests passing, clippy clean, cargo fmt clean.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): resolve 108 CI test failures from feat(demo-login)

Root cause 1: Strict mode violations - 'Login with Demo' button text
matches /login/i, making unauthenticated-redirect assertions resolve to
2 elements. Add .first() in 8 spec files.

Root cause 2: DEMO_PASSWORD not set in CI and demo@ekost.app user
missing. Add DEMO_PASSWORD to ci.yml env and register demo user in
auth.setup.ts using an isolated browser context.

Root cause 3: Session cascade - demo-login test timeouts (~180s overhead)
pushed the test run past the 5-minute cookieCache window. After cache
expiry, the DB session deleted by logout.spec.ts caused all subsequent
tests to fail. Fix by registering a dedicated logout user in auth.setup.ts
and updating logout.spec.ts to use user-logout.json, isolating session
invalidation from the main E2E session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- discover_subscriptions MCP tool was using INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN (admin)
- This returned no subscriptions (empty array) for partner API key callers
- Partners couldn't discover their available subscriptions/feeds

Solution:
- Pass partner ID (callerId) to listAvailableSubscriptions method
- Use x-partner-id header when partner context is available
- Fall back to INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN only when no partner context
- Subscription service already filters by partner (shows where they're subscriber or provider)

Changes:
- apps/exchange-agent/src/services/provisioningClient.ts:
  - Updated listAvailableSubscriptions to accept optional partnerId parameter
  - If partnerId provided, use x-partner-id header for partner-scoped filtering
  - If no partnerId, use INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN for admin query

- apps/exchange-agent/src/routes/mcp.ts:
  - Pass callerId (partner ID) to listAvailableSubscriptions
  - Subscriptions now filtered by calling partner's context

Result:
- Partners with API keys now see subscriptions where they are subscriber or provider
- Admin operations continue to work (when no partner context)
- Proper authorization and context filtering via partner ID
